findings.message: record_raw_path() stores one map entry per raw full path (including the query string) with no sanitize, no distinct-value cap, and no 'other' overflow bucket. Every other metric in this class routes through _san()/_bump_capped() (MAX_ROUTES, MAX_ATTRIBUTION, MAX_REFERRERS) or a fixed enum; this path is unbounded. A public RUM beacon endpoint can mint an unlimited number of distinct full_path values, producing unbounded label cardinality that will blow up the Prometheus series count.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize the path (strip the query string, normalize via _san) and route it through _bump_capped(self._raw_paths, path, self.MAX_ROUTES, overflow='other') so the label degrades into an 'other' bucket once the cap is hit, matching record_page_view().

findings.message: app_frontend_raw_path_total emits one time series per raw full path with no sanitize and no cap, so the path="..." label is unbounded / high-cardinality. Combined with record_raw_path() above, an attacker or normal query-string variation creates an unbounded number of series in /metrics.
findings.suggestion: Apply the same distinct-value cap + 'other' overflow used for page_views/attribution/referrers before rendering, so path is effectively a bounded enum. Alternatively drop this metric entirely since app_frontend_page_views_total already covers route-level page views.
